summary: Matched a block of Donkey Kong fighter code by moving roughly 0x8010DB3C-0x8010DFF8 behavior out of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftdonkey_2.s into C across ftdonkey2.c and a new ftdonkey3.c. The work covered Donkey Kong SpecialLw action startup and action callbacks, updated the Donkey action-state table to point at ftDonkey_* C symbols, exposed newly understood special-attribute fields, and added the new object file to the build.

key_files.role: Removed matched assembly for Donkey Kong functions and changed as_table_donkey callback entries from lbl_/func_ symbols to ftDonkey_* C symbols; retained later unmatched asm.
Added C implementation of ftDonkey_8010DB3C, including throw-flag handling, collision probing with func_80056C54, Vec fallback to fighter position, y offset, and x914 vector updates.
New source file containing ftDonkey_SpecialLw_StartAction and related callbacks ftDonkey_8010DCD8 through ftDonkey_8010DFF8.
Expanded ftDonkeyAttributes to expose fields at x68, x6C, and x70; added prototypes for ftDonkey_8010DB3C, ftDonkey_8010DE88, and ftDonkey_8010DFF8.
Updated commented prototypes from func_8010DE88/func_8010DFF8 to ftDonkey_8010DE88/ftDonkey_8010DFF8.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftDonkey/ftdonkey3.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES so the new matched C compiles into the build.

what_changed: Removed 399 lines from ftdonkey_2.s and added 175 lines across C/header/build files, indicating a substantial assembly-to-C match for Donkey Kong code.

Added ftDonkey_8010DB3C in ftdonkey2.c to handle a Donkey throw/accessory callback: it checks fighter->x2210_ThrowFlags.b3, clears it, checks colldata->x134_envFlags & 0x18000, loops over four vectors, probes ground/collision via func_80056C54, falls back to fighter->xB0_pos when the probe fails, raises y by 2.0f, and writes vectors with func_8007B8A8(&fighter->x914[i], ...).

Created ftdonkey3.c with C implementations for ftDonkey_SpecialLw_StartAction, multiple animation/IASA/physics/collision callbacks, and action transitions using Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC for action IDs 0x17F, 0x180, and 0x181.

Moved callback table references in as_table_donkey from anonymous assembly names like lbl_8010DCD8 and func_8010DF5C to ftDonkey_8010DCD8, ftDonkey_8010DD74, ftDonkey_8010DF5C, and related ftDonkey_* names.

Split ftDonkey_8010DE88 behavior by adding ftDonkey_8010DE88_inner, which handles effect spawning with efAsync_Spawn, hitlag effect callbacks efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll, and x2219_flag.bits.b0 guarding.
Updated Donkey special attributes by replacing filler through 0x71 with filler to 0x68 plus f32 x68, f32 x6C, and f32 x70, matching the fields used by ftDonkey_8010DB3C.
Removed local sdata2 float constants from asm for 1.5f, 2.0f, 0.0f, 1.0f, and the int-to-double conversion constant; inserted a .balign 8 before the remaining lbl_804D95D0.
smart_moves: Kept the action-state table in asm but retargeted entries to C function symbols, allowing partial decompilation without needing to move the whole table out of assembly.
Introduced a new ftdonkey3.c and added it to obj_files.mk instead of overloading the existing ftdonkey2.c with all newly matched SpecialLw callbacks.
Exposed only the ftDonkeyAttributes fields needed for the matched code at x68, x6C, and x70 while retaining filler for unknown earlier bytes.
Used explicit ftDonkey_* prefixes for previously anonymous labels/functions, making table callbacks searchable and character-scoped.
Preserved matching-sensitive local stack artifacts with comments such as unused[2] and 'can't remove with get inlines', showing deliberate handling of compiler/codegen constraints.
Factored repeated post-action setup into ftDonkey_8010DE88_inner, matching the apparent asm structure while keeping the outer action-change routine readable.
decomp_lessons: When matching fighter action callbacks, update both the C implementation and the asm action-state table symbol references in the same change; otherwise tables can continue to point at removed labels.
For character-specific callbacks with unknown semantic names, use the project convention ftCharacter_address rather than leaving lbl_ or generic func_ names in tables.
Special-attribute structs can be incrementally refined: replace filler only at offsets proven by loads/stores in matched functions, and leave unknown regions as filler.
Build integration is part of matching: adding a new C source file requires updating obj_files.mk, especially when replacing assembly code in the same text ordering region.
Small dummy locals or unused stack arrays may be necessary to reproduce stack layout and match old MWCC output; document them so future cleanup does not break matching.
Literal floats in matched C can replace sdata2 labels, but remaining assembly data may need alignment preservation such as .balign 8.
naming_conventions: Renamed anonymous Donkey callbacks from lbl_8010DCD8/lbl_8010DD14/lbl_8010DD18/lbl_8010DD38 to ftDonkey_8010DCD8/ftDonkey_8010DD14/ftDonkey_8010DD18/ftDonkey_8010DD38.
Renamed generic Donkey functions from func_8010DF5C/func_8010DF98/func_8010DF9C/func_8010DFBC to ftDonkey_8010DF5C/ftDonkey_8010DF98/ftDonkey_8010DF9C/ftDonkey_8010DFBC.
Kept the public semantic name ftDonkey_SpecialLw_StartAction for the grounded Donkey down-special start action.
Used address-suffixed names for still-unknown behavior, e.g. ftDonkey_8010DB3C, ftDonkey_8010DE88, and ftDonkey_8010DFF8.
Named newly identified attribute fields by offset, x68/x6C/x70, rather than inventing semantic names without stronger evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted a four-iteration assembly loop into a for (i = 0; i < 4; i++) loop over Vec vec_list[4], with pointer arithmetic expressed through fighter->x914[i].
Preserved stack/codegen shape using unused local arrays in ftDonkey_8010DB3C and ftDonkey_8010DCD8/ftDonkey_8010DF5C.
Matched bitfield operations through existing Fighter fields: x2210_ThrowFlags.b3, x2210_ThrowFlags.flags, x2219_flag.bits.b0, input.x668 & 0x200, and x2340_stateVar1.
Expressed action transitions with Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC using NULL and literal floats 0.0f/1.0f rather than retaining sdata2 labels.
Represented callback writes directly through fighter->cb fields: x21EC_callback, x21D4_callback_EnterHitlag, x21D8_callback_ExitHitlag, and x21BC_callback_Accessory4.
Maintained sdata2 alignment after deleting constants by adding .balign 8 before lbl_804D95D0.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text body repeats the title: 'matched some donkey kong functions', so rationale beyond the diff is weak.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftDonkey_8010DE88, ftDonkey_8010DFF8, or other ftDonkey_8010D* callbacks to semantic SpecialLw names.
Search for subsequent changes to ftDonkeyAttributes x68/x6C/x70 to determine their semantic meaning.
Look for PRs that move as_table_donkey fully from assembly to C or define Donkey action-state enum names for IDs 0x17F, 0x180, and 0x181.
Search for matching patterns using unused[2] or dummy stack locals in fighter callback decompilation.
Check whether ftDonkey_8010DE88_inner remains factored or is later merged/renamed during cleanup.
